title: Sensor Types
description: Compare Plantgotchi's four sensor models and find the right one for your setup.

sections.body: Plantgotchi offers four sensor models, each designed for different setups. All sensors measure the same core metrics: soil moisture, temperature, light, and battery level.

sections.heading: BLE Child Sensor — $9–$12
sections.body:
The tiny one. A Bluetooth-only sensor powered by a CR2032 coin cell battery. It lasts up to 2 years and is perfect for pairing with a hub or parent sensor. Since it's BLE-only, it needs a nearby phone, Solo sensor (parent mode), or PlantCam Pro to relay its data.

  • BLE only — no WiFi
  • CR2032 battery, ~2-year life
  • Smallest and cheapest option

sections.heading: Solo Sensor — $15–$19
sections.body:
The independent one. Has both WiFi and BLE, so it can upload data directly to the cloud without needing a phone nearby. USB-C rechargeable with an optional OLED screen that shows current readings.

  • WiFi + BLE connectivity
  • USB-C rechargeable
  • Optional OLED display
  • Can act as a parent for BLE Child sensors

sections.heading: PlantCam — $25–$29
sections.body:
The photographer. Adds a 5MP camera for automatic time-lapse photos of your plant's growth. Takes a photo every few hours and stores it locally. Battery lasts 4–6 months.

  • 5MP camera with auto time-lapse
  • 4–6 month battery life
  • All standard sensor readings included

sections.heading: PlantCam Pro — $69–$79
sections.body:
The brain. Built on a Raspberry Pi, this is the hub for serious plant collectors. It runs AI plant disease detection on-device, acts as a central hub for all your BLE Child sensors, and connects everything to the cloud.

  • AI plant disease detection
  • Hub for unlimited BLE Child sensors
  • Raspberry Pi powered
  • Always-on (USB-C powered)
